canonical_name: the country beyond the world's end
the friend who was dearest to him in all the world save only Arthur's self
all the kings and knights of her day
the boat that would bring them to the island
a barge draped in black, with a dark figure at the prow
the little Gwenhwyfar who had been lessoned here when she was a child
a single one of his knights
a few of the renegade Northmen
a land that ran red with blood, where sons were not content to await their crowning day
the shores of the Lake where they challenged each other
one who had no fear of any Goddess or any God
the sacred waters of the Well
others standing there with me, robed and crowned
the Mother who had been torn asunder when Gwydion was bom
the Death-crone who stood at my side
the four fair queens in the sunrise
the Northmen who were the new foe
the little child Igraine had put into my arms
that Joseph of Arimathea who had come here from the Holy Land
all the old Companions of the Round Table
One of the monks who carried him
